Patient Name:        Margaret Tran
Date of Birth:       [on file]
Sex:                 Female
Ordering Provider:   Thoracic Oncology Service
Clinical Indication: Non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma, stage IV вҖ” evaluation for acquired resistance to osimertinib (TAGRISSO)

Specimen ID:         MT-ADR-RES-02
Specimen Type:       Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy
Primary Specimen:    Right adrenal gland metastasis (CT-guided biopsy)
Concurrent Specimen: Plasma cell-free DNA (liquid biopsy, 20 mL EDTA)
Tumor Cellularity:   Estimated 55% by H&E review (adequate)
Pathologic Dx:       Metastatic lung adenocarcinoma; TTF-1 positive (confirmed on concurrent pathology report)
Prior NGS on File:   Yes вҖ” baseline tumor panel from right lower lobe primary (see prior report, Specimen ID MT-RLL-DX-01)

Methodology:
Tissue: 523-gene hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing panel (tumor-normal paired analysis). Sequencing performed on Illumina NovaSeq platform. Mean coverage: 750x (tumor), 200x (normal germline). Minimum reportable somatic allele frequency: 2.0%. Copy number analysis by normalized read-depth ratio. Structural variant/fusion detection via paired-end read analysis. TMB calculated from somatic synonymous and non-synonymous mutations per megabase across coding regions (1.1 Mb). MSI determined by analysis of 95 microsatellite loci.
Liquid Biopsy: Plasma cfDNA extracted from 20 mL peripheral blood (EDTA). Digital droplet PCR (ddPCR) and targeted error-correction sequencing (TECS) used for EGFR hotspot quantification; validated to limit of detection ~0.05% allele fraction for tier 1 variants.

INTERPRETATION
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
This molecular profile is obtained from a metastatic right adrenal lesion at the time of clinical and radiographic progression following approximately 14 months of first-line osimertinib (TAGRISSO) therapy for EGFR L858R-mutant stage IIIB/IV lung adenocarcinoma.

The dominant finding is the ACQUISITION of an EGFR p.T790M (exon 20) mutation at a variant allele frequency of 19.4%, which was absent in the pre-treatment baseline specimen. This is the most well-characterized mechanism of acquired resistance to third-generation EGFR TKIs, including osimertinib, and occurs in approximately 10вҖ“15% of patients progressing on this agent. Mechanistically, T790M in the context of prior osimertinib treatment reflects re-emergence of a resistant subclone вҖ” osimertinib's irreversible binding to the mutant EGFR kinase is circumvented through a conformational change at the gatekeeper residue, partially restoring wild-type-like ATP affinity. The retained EGFR L858R driver mutation (28.1% VAF) confirms ongoing EGFR pathway dependence of the dominant clone.

Critically, EGFR C797S вҖ” a tertiary mutation that would abolish covalent binding of all currently approved EGFR TKIs and most investigational agents вҖ” is NOT detected in either tissue or plasma. This is an important negative finding that preserves potential therapeutic options targeting EGFR in the cis or trans configuration.

Also of note is the subclonal, newly acquired PIK3CA p.E545K mutation (8.3% VAF), detected in tissue but below plasma sensitivity threshold. Subclonal PI3K pathway activation has been described as a secondary resistance mechanism following EGFR TKI exposure, potentially permitting EGFR-independent proliferative signaling through AKT/mTOR. At its present low clonal fraction, this is not currently a dominant resistance driver but warrants monitoring with serial ctDNA.

The newly acquired CDK4 amplification (CN 6.2), in the setting of pre-existing CDKN2A heterozygous deletion (retained from baseline), suggests convergent deregulation of the G1/S checkpoint. CDK4 amplification has been reported as an acquired resistance mechanism across multiple oncogene-driven NSCLC contexts. Whether CDK4/6 inhibition would be clinically useful in this setting remains investigational.

High-level MET amplification вҖ” one of the most clinically important EGFR-bypass resistance mechanisms (present in ~5вҖ“10% of osimertinib progressors) вҖ” is NOT present. MET gain is borderline (CN 3.8) and below the accepted amplification threshold, consistent across tissue and plasma. This is relevant because MET bypass typically predicts sensitivity to combined EGFR/MET inhibition strategies.

No fusion-based bypass mechanisms (RET, NTRK, FGFR, NRG1, etc.) were identified.

TMB remains low (5.8 mut/Mb) and MSS status is confirmed, consistent with the baseline profile and the known biology of EGFR-driven lung adenocarcinoma. Immunotherapy monotherapy is unlikely to provide meaningful benefit in this molecular context.

In summary, the predominant acquired resistance mechanism to osimertinib in this patient is EGFR T790M, with secondary subclonal alterations in PIK3CA and CDK4 potentially contributing. The absence of high-level MET amplification, ERBB2 amplification, C797S, and fusion-based bypass mechanisms are all clinically meaningful negative findings.

  TIER I (Standard of Care / Guideline-Supported):
    - EGFR T790M (acquired): Historically targeted by osimertinib, but T790M here
      represents on-target resistance TO osimertinib rather than T790M in a first-
      generation TKI-resistant patient. Standard second-line options for post-
      osimertinib T790M are not well-defined; platinum-doublet chemotherapy represents
      the current guideline-concordant approach for systemic progression.

  TIER II (Investigational / Clinical Trial Priority):
    - EGFR L858R + T790M (post-osimertinib, C797S-negative): Potentially targetable by
      bispecific EGFR/MET antibody combinations (e.g., amivantamab-based regimens),
      fourth-generation EGFR TKI approaches (e.g., BLU-701, BBT-176), or allosteric
      EGFR inhibitors. Clinical trial enrollment strongly encouraged.
    - PIK3CA E545K (subclonal): PI3K/AKT pathway co-targeting (alpelisib, capivasertib)
      in combination with EGFR-directed agents вҖ” investigational.
    - CDK4 amplification: CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) in
      EGFR-mutant NSCLC вҖ” investigational; no established standard indication.

  TIER IV (Not Actionable at This Time):
    - TP53 p.R175H: No approved targeted therapy; prognostic co-mutation only.
    - MET low-level gain (CN 3.8): Below amplification threshold; not actionable.
    - ERBB2 low-level gain (CN 3.1): Below HER2 amplification threshold; no
      activating mutation; not actionable.
    - MSS, low TMB: Immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy not supported.
